zh.Stonyhurst, Clitheroe, Lancashire, Engla: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾斯托爾斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Mabel de Knoll於1335年出生在英國藍卡爾其皮三時，她的父親Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace39歲，母親Margaret de Singleton24歲。她和Sir. John de Bayley至少有1個兒子。她去世於從1403年1月10日到1404年1月9日在法國皮卡迪索姆。

zh-hans.Stonyhurst, Clitheroe, Lancashire, Engla: 英国英格兰藍卡爾斯托爾斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mabel de Knoll于1335年出生在英国藍卡爾其皮三时，她的父亲Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace39岁，母亲Margaret de Singleton24岁。她和Sir. John de Bayley至少有1个儿子。她去世于从1403年1月10日到1404年1月9日在法国皮卡迪索姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Knoll
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: topographic name for someone living near a hilltop, from Middle English knolle ‘hilltop, hillock’ (Old English cnoll), Middle High German knol ‘peak’. In some cases the English name is habitational, from one of the many places called with this word, for example Knole in Kent or Knowle in Dorset, West Midlands, and elsewhere.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a peasant or a crude, clumsy person, from Middle High German knolle ‘lump, clod’, German Knolle.

variants.name: Knowler
meanings: English (Kent and Sussex): topographic name from Middle English knowle ‘knoll, hilltop’ + the locative suffix -er. See also Knoll .

variants.name: Kniola
meanings: Polish (Knioła): adaptation of German Knoll , Knolle .

variants.name: Knolle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Ernst.

variants.name: Knoth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Frieda, Georg.
German: nickname for a gnarled or crude person, from Middle High German knode, knote ‘knot’. Compare Knoll , Knorr .

variants.name: Knull
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhard, Kurt, Manfred.

variants.name: Knollman
meanings: Americanized form of German Knollmann: topographic name for someone living near the summit of a hill or mountain (see Knoll 1).

nameMeanings.name: Mabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname from the Old French vocabulary word amabel, amable ‘lovely’ (akin to modern English amiable ‘friendly, good-humoured’). The initial vowel began to be lost as early as the 12th century (the same woman is referred to as both Mabilia and Amabilia in a document of 1185 ), but a short vowel in the resulting first syllable was standard, giving a rhyme with babble, until the 19th century, when people began to pronounce the name to rhyme with table.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Mabel de Knoll was born in 1335, in Chipping, Lancashire, England, her father, Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace, was 39 and her mother, Margaret de Singleton, was 24. She had at least 1 son with Sir. John de Bayley. She died from 10 January 1403 to 9 January 1404, in Somme, Picardie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mabel de Knoll est née en 1335, à Chipping, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Singleton, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Sir. John de Bayley. Elle est décédée du 10 janvier 1403 au 9 janvier 1404, à Somme, Picardie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mabel de Knoll 1335 in Chipping, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace 39 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Singleton 24. Sie hatte mit Sir. John de Bayley mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb von 10. Januar 1403 bis 9. Januar 1404 in Somme, Picardie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mabel de Knoll è nata il nel 1335 nel Chipping, Lancashire, England, sua padre, Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Singleton, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Sir. John de Bayley. Morì da 10 gennaio 1403 a 9 gennaio 1404, nel Somme, Picardie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Mabel de Knollが1335年にイギリスランカシュアイアチップイングで生まれた時，父Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peaceは39歳，母Margaret de Singletonは24歳であった。彼女はSir. John de Bayleyとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女はから1403年1月10日まで1404年1月9日にフランスピカーディソンメで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Mabel de Knoll(이)가 1335년 영국 랭카실르 치핀그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Singleton(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 Sir. John de Bayley(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1403년 1월 10일부터 1404년 1월 9일까지 프랑스 피카디 솜에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mabel de Knoll nasceu em 1335, em Chipping, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Singleton, tinha 24 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Sir. John de Bayley. Ela faleceu de 10 de janeiro de 1403 a 9 de janeiro de 1404, em Somme, Picardie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mabel de Knoll родилась в 1335 г. в местности чиппинг, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Lord Thomas de Knoll Commission of the Peace было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Singleton, 24. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Sir. John de Bayley. Она умерла с 10 января 1403 по 9 января 1404 в местности сомм, пикарди, Франция.
